researched_category.notes: FanBasis is an all-in-one platform for digital businesses, offering payment processing, subscriptions, checkout, affiliates, analytics, and community management tools for creators and entrepreneurs.

one_liner.value: All-in-one payment and business infrastructure for digital creators and entrepreneurs to sell, host, and scale online products and services.

methods.notes: Authentication is done via an API key passed in the x-api-key header. No OAuth2, Basic auth, or other methods are mentioned in the API docs.

access.notes: SourceForge lists pricing starting at Free with a free version available. Signup is self-serve via email/password or Google/Apple. Phone verification is required during signup. API keys are generated from the dashboard after account creation. However, the main website prominently features 'Book a Demo Call' suggesting some sales-led onboarding for enterprise features.

summary.value: Comprehensive REST API documented at apidocs.fan covering checkout sessions, payments, subscriptions, customers, products, webhooks, discounts, transactions, and events. Sandbox/QA environment available. Official TypeScript SDK (@fanbasis/checkout-sdk on npm) and SDKs for Node, Python, PHP, Ruby. Postman collection available. 10,000+ integrations via Zapier.
summary.notes: The API is well-documented with an interactive playground. Endpoints are served under /public-api/ path. The subscription proration API is under /api/seller/v1/.

agent_interface.notes: No official or community MCP server found for FanBasis. No Composio toolkit integration found. An official TypeScript SDK (@fanbasis/checkout-sdk) exists on npm.

rationale.value: FanBasis has a well-documented public REST API with API key authentication that can be self-served (free tier available). The API has broad coverage of the platform's functionality including payments, subscriptions, customers, webhooks, and products. An official TypeScript SDK exists. While no MCP server exists yet, a custom MCP server can be built using the documented API. The main drawback is that there is no existing MCP server or Composio integration, but the API is straightforward enough to build an agent toolkit around.

s1.quote: The Operating System for Modern Digital Businesses. All-in-one software to run high-ticket, high-margin digital businesses. Everything from checkout to fulfillment with built-in financing, premium payments, and enterprise-grade scale.
s1.title: FanBasis Homepage

s2.quote: FanBasis is an all-in-one software platform designed to run high-ticket, high-margin digital businesses, providing everything from checkout to fulfillment with built-in financing, premium payments, and enterprise-grade scale.
s2.title: FanBasis Reviews on SourceForge

s3.quote: REST API + full documentation — comprehensive reference, Postman collection, and real-time webhooks. SDKs for Node, Python, PHP, Ruby — idiomatic libraries maintained by our engineering team.
s3.title: FanBasis Enterprise

s4.quote: Every API request must include your API key in the x-api-key header. There are no usernames or passwords — just your key. Every endpoint is served under the /public-api/ path on https://www.fanbasis.com.
s4.title: FanBasis API Reference

s5.quote: Log into your FanBasis dashboard and go to the API Keys section. Copy your API key. You'll use it in every request you make.
s5.title: FanBasis API Reference - Quick Start

s6.quote: Pricing for FanBasis starts at Free. Free Version: Free Version available.
s6.title: FanBasis Pricing on SourceForge

s7.quote: Sign up as a seller... Email Address, Create Password, Re-enter Password, Your Website. Also phone verification is required: 'A verification code has been sent to your phone.'
s7.title: FanBasis Signup Page

s8.quote: New accounts may undergo a brief review period before payouts are enabled.
s8.title: FanBasis API Reference - Account Reviews

s9.quote: The official TypeScript library for the Fanbasis Checkout API. Repository: https://github.com/FanBasis/fanbasis-sdk
s9.title: @fanbasis/checkout-sdk on npm

s10.quote: The FanBasis API lets you build custom integrations with your FanBasis account. Instead of managing everything manually through the dashboard, you can write code that automatically creates payment pages, tracks who paid, manages subscriptions, and reacts to events in real time.
s10.title: FanBasis API Reference - Introduction

s11.quote: Composio is an integration platform that provides access to 500+ tools across popular applications like GitHub, Slack, Notion, and more.
s11.title: Composio Toolkits (no FanBasis found)

limitations: Could not confirm exact pricing tiers (the pricing page returned an error). SourceForge indicates a free version exists.
The signup flow includes phone verification, which adds some friction.
No MCP server or Composio integration exists yet, but the API itself is well-documented and buildable.
The platform appears to have some sales-led onboarding (Book a Demo Call CTA) which may indicate gating for certain features.
